---
title: "ECLIPS Internal Workshop 2"
author: "Frank DiTraglia & Ludovica Gazze"
institute: "Preliminary Results"
format: 
  beamer:
    aspectratio: 169
    theme: default
    header-includes: |
      \setbeamertemplate{footline}{\hfill\insertframenumber\,/\,\inserttotalframenumber\hspace{2em}\vspace{1ex}} 
      \usepackage{tikz}
      \usetikzlibrary{positioning}
      \usetikzlibrary{arrows}
      \usetikzlibrary{shapes}
      \usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta}
      \usetikzlibrary{fit}
      \usepackage{booktabs}
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(tidyverse)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
                      dev = "png", dpi = 300, fig.align = "center")

# Shared constants: colors, treatment labels, gradient palettes
COL_BLUE    <- "#3B6E8F"   # primary
COL_ORANGE  <- "#B5651D"   # secondary
COL_ORANGE2 <- "#C46A1B"   # kit-type accent
TREAT_LABELS <- c(C = "Control", G = "Generalized", P = "Personalized")
AGE_COLS   <- c("Old" = COL_ORANGE, "New" = COL_BLUE, "Unknown" = "#7A7A7A")
TREAT_COLS <- c("Control" = "#666666", "Generalized" = COL_BLUE,
                "Personalized" = COL_ORANGE)

raw_all <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/combined-pseudonymized.csv",
                    show_col_types = FALSE)

# Baseline response outcome: response within 45 days of the round's mailing
# (Round 1: 2025-11-14 -> 2025-12-29; Round 2: 2026-01-22 -> 2026-03-08).
# Round 1's survey stayed open until 2026-01-05; the 2 responses (1 usable)
# received in the gap are excluded from every count and comparison below.
raw <- raw_all |>
  filter(!between(as.Date(EndDate),
                  as.Date("2025-12-30"), as.Date("2026-01-21")))

# Usable respondents only, with ordinal demographics coded as ordered factors
# (short display labels for the long survey categories).
surv <- raw |>
  filter(usable) |>
  mutate(
    prior_lead_knowledge = factor(prior_lead_knowledge,
      levels = c("Yes", "No", "Not sure")),
    worried_exposure = factor(worried_exposure,
      levels = c("Not worried", "A little worried", "Quite worried",
                 "Very worried", "Not sure", "Rather not say")),
    income = factor(income,
      levels = c("Under £15,000", "£15,000 - £24,999", "£25,000 - £34,999",
                 "£35,000 - £49,999", "£50,000 - £74,999", "£75,000 - £99,999",
                 "£100,000 - £149,999", "£150,000 and above",
                 "Not sure", "Rather not say")),
    highest_education = factor(highest_education,
      levels = c("No formal qualifications",
                 "Education up to age 16 (GCSE or equivalent)",
                 "Education up to age 18 (A-levels, high school diploma, baccalaureate, or equivalent)",
                 "Post-school qualification (non-degree) - e.g., diplomas, certificates, apprenticeships or other vocational training",
                 "Undergraduate degree (Bachelor's or equivalent)",
                 "Postgraduate degree (Master's, PhD or equivalent)",
                 "Rather not say"),
      labels = c("No formal quals", "Up to age 16 (GCSE)", "Up to age 18 (A-level)",
                 "Post-school (non-degree)", "Undergraduate", "Postgraduate",
                 "Rather not say")),
    home = factor(home,
      levels = c("Owned by you (either with a mortgage or outright)",
                 "Rented from the council", "Rented from a private landlord",
                 "Living with family or friends", "Other:", "Not sure",
                 "Rather not say"),
      labels = c("Owned", "Rented (council)", "Rented (private)",
                 "With family/friends", "Other", "Not sure", "Rather not say")),
    ethnicity = factor(ethnicity,
      levels = c("White (British [English, Welsh, Scottish, Northern Irish], Irish, Gypsy or Irish Traveller, Roma, any other White background)",
                 "Asian or Asian British (Indian, Pakistani, Bangladeshi, Chinese, any other Asian background)",
                 "Black, Black British, Caribbean or African (Caribbean, African, any other Black background)",
                 "Mixed or multiple backgrounds (White and Black Caribbean, White and Black African, White and Asian, any other mixed background)",
                 "Other (Arab, any other ethnicity)",
                 "Rather not say"),
      labels = c("White", "Asian / Asian British", "Black / Black British",
                 "Mixed", "Other", "Rather not say"))
  )

# Cell-level usable-response rates (mirrors 2026-04-16 combined analysis §2.3/§3):
# denominator = letters mailed per cell; rate per 1,000 with Wilson score CI.
cell_levels <- c("NonOld_C", "NonOld_G", "Old_C", "Old_G", "Old_P")
ci_level   <- 0.95                                 # CI level used everywhere below
ci_pct     <- paste0(round(100 * ci_level), "%")   # e.g. "95%"
ci_caption <- paste0("Bars: ", ci_pct, " Wilson score intervals")

# Wilson score interval bounds, as proportions (= prop.test, no cc).
wilson_lohi <- function(x, n, cl = ci_level) {
  z <- qnorm(1 - (1 - cl) / 2); p <- x / n
  ctr <- (p + z^2 / (2 * n)) / (1 + z^2 / n)
  h   <- z * sqrt(p * (1 - p) / n + z^2 / (4 * n^2)) / (1 + z^2 / n)
  c(lo = ctr - h, hi = ctr + h)
}

# Point = observed rate (x/n) with Wilson bounds; scale = 1000 for per-1,000
# rates, 100 for percent.
score_ci <- function(x, n, cl = 0.95, scale = 1000) {
  w <- wilson_lohi(x, n, cl)
  c(est = scale * x / n, lo = scale * w[["lo"]], hi = scale * w[["hi"]])
}

# Attach est/lo/hi columns for x successes of n trials (Wilson, ci_level).
add_score_ci <- function(df, x, n, scale = 1000) {
  df |>
    rowwise() |>
    mutate(ci = list(score_ci({{ x }}, {{ n }}, cl = ci_level, scale = scale))) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(est = map_dbl(ci, "est"), lo = map_dbl(ci, "lo"),
           hi = map_dbl(ci, "hi")) |>
    select(-ci)
}

# Newcombe (1998) hybrid score interval for a difference of proportions, built
# from the two Wilson intervals. Returns proportions (not per-1,000).
score_diff <- function(x1, n1, x0, n0) {
  p1 <- x1 / n1; p0 <- x0 / n0; w1 <- wilson_lohi(x1, n1); w0 <- wilson_lohi(x0, n0)
  d <- p1 - p0
  tibble(diff = d,
         lo = unname(d - sqrt((p1 - w1["lo"])^2 + (w0["hi"] - p0)^2)),
         hi = unname(d + sqrt((w1["hi"] - p1)^2 + (p0 - w0["lo"])^2)))
}

# --- Input files, each read once; everything below derives from these ---

# addr: one row per mailed address (both rounds, 150k). age_grp splits the
# design's Non-Old into New (known >= 1967) vs Unknown (no EPC record).
addr <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/addresses-and-treatments.csv",
                 show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(cell = paste(house_type, treatment, sep = "_"),
         age_grp = case_when(house_type == "Old"              ~ "Old",
                             !is.na(age_end) & age_end > 1966 ~ "New",
                             TRUE                             ~ "Unknown"))

lsoa_ref <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/lsoa-reference-table.csv",
                     show_col_types = FALSE)
lsoa_imd  <- lsoa_ref |> select(lsoa = lsoa21cd, imd_score)
lsoa_elig <- lsoa_ref |> select(lsoa = lsoa21cd, E_l)

# The same Old/New/Unknown split, from the response side's age_status column.
age_grp_from_status <- function(x) {
  case_match(x, "Known Non-Old" ~ "New", "Unknown Age" ~ "Unknown", .default = x)
}

mailed_per_cell <- addr |> count(cell, name = "n_mailed")

# Quantities for the Phase I flowchart (interpolated into the tikz below).
fmt <- function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",")
n_mailed_total <- sum(mailed_per_cell$n_mailed)
n_started      <- nrow(raw)
n_usable       <- sum(raw$usable)

cell_table <- mailed_per_cell |>
  left_join(
    raw |>
      mutate(cell = paste(house_type_parsed, treatment_parsed, sep = "_")) |>
      group_by(cell) |>
      summarise(n_responded = n(), n_usable = sum(usable), .groups = "drop"),
    by = "cell"
  ) |>
  add_score_ci(n_usable, n_mailed) |>
  mutate(cell = factor(cell, levels = cell_levels)) |>
  arrange(cell)

# Forest plot of per-cell rates (est/lo/hi): Old/Non-Old rows, house colour.
cell_forest <- function(df, xlab) {
  df |>
    mutate(
      house  = if_else(str_starts(as.character(cell), "Old"), "Old", "Non-Old"),
      letter = unname(TREAT_LABELS[str_sub(as.character(cell), start = -1L)]),
      label  = factor(paste0(house, ": ", letter),
                      levels = rev(c("Non-Old: Control", "Non-Old: Generalized",
                                     "Old: Control", "Old: Generalized",
                                     "Old: Personalized")))
    ) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = est, y = label, colour = house)) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi), height = 0.22, linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("Old" = COL_ORANGE, "Non-Old" = COL_BLUE)) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = NULL, colour = NULL, caption = ci_caption) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
          plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
}

# --- IMD (deprivation) gradient: usable rate by IMD quintile ---
# imd_score higher = more deprived; quintiles = equal fifths of the 488 Leeds LSOAs.
addr_imd <- addr |>
  select(lsoa, treatment, age_grp) |>
  left_join(lsoa_imd, by = "lsoa")

imd_breaks <- quantile(lsoa_imd$imd_score, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.2), na.rm = TRUE)
bin_imd <- function(x) cut(x, imd_breaks, include.lowest = TRUE,
                           labels = c("Q1\nleast", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5\nmost"))

imd_cells <- addr_imd |>
  mutate(q = bin_imd(imd_score)) |>
  filter(!is.na(q)) |>
  count(q, age_grp, treatment, name = "mailed") |>
  left_join(
    raw |>
      mutate(age_grp = age_grp_from_status(age_status)) |>
      select(lsoa, treatment = treatment_parsed, age_grp, usable) |>
      left_join(lsoa_imd, by = "lsoa") |>
      mutate(q = bin_imd(imd_score)) |>
      filter(!is.na(q)) |>
      group_by(q, age_grp, treatment) |>
      summarise(usable = sum(usable), clicks = n(), .groups = "drop"),
    by = c("q", "age_grp", "treatment")
  ) |>
  mutate(across(c(usable, clicks), \(x) replace_na(x, 0L)))

# rate per 1,000 + Wilson CI for an IMD-quintile grouping; outcome = usable or clicks.
imd_summary <- function(df, extra = character(0), outcome = "usable") {
  df |>
    group_by(across(all_of(c("q", extra)))) |>
    summarise(mailed = sum(mailed), num = sum(.data[[outcome]]), .groups = "drop") |>
    add_score_ci(num, mailed) |>
    rename(rate = est)
}

# IMD-quintile gradient: line + pointrange per colour group (rate/lo/hi).
gradient_plot <- function(df, colour_var, palette, ylab, caption = ci_caption) {
  ggplot(df, aes(x = q, y = rate, colour = .data[[colour_var]],
                 group = .data[[colour_var]])) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), size = 0.45,
                    position = position_dodge(0.12)) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = palette) +
    labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)", y = ylab, colour = NULL,
         caption = caption) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
          plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 8, b = 4, l = 6))
}

# --- Map data: Leeds LSOA (2021) boundaries + per-LSOA quantities ---
# Boundaries from ONS (BGC, OGL v3); see ../data/leeds-lsoa-2021-bgc-PROVENANCE.md.
library(sf)
leeds_geo <- st_read("../data/leeds-lsoa-2021-bgc.geojson", quiet = TRUE)

# Leeds LSOA choropleth (continuous fill); df carries an lsoa column.
lsoa_map <- function(df, col, legend, option = "mako") {
  leeds_geo |>
    left_join(df, by = c("LSOA21CD" = "lsoa")) |>
    ggplot(aes(fill = .data[[col]])) +
    geom_sf(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.03) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = option, direction = -1, name = legend,
                         na.value = "grey85") +
    coord_sf(datum = NA) +
    theme_void(base_size = 13) +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
}

# Letters mailed per LSOA across both rounds (addr_imd = one row per mailed address).
letters_by_lsoa <- addr_imd |> count(lsoa, name = "n_letters")

# Empirical-Bayes (Beta-Binomial) shrinkage of per-LSOA usable-response rates.
# y usable of n letters per LSOA; p ~ Beta(a, b) with (a, b) by marginal MLE.
eb_lsoa <- letters_by_lsoa |>
  rename(n = n_letters) |>
  left_join(raw |> filter(usable) |> count(lsoa, name = "y"), by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(y = replace_na(y, 0L))
eb_nll <- function(p) {
  a <- exp(p[1]); b <- exp(p[2])
  -sum(lchoose(eb_lsoa$n, eb_lsoa$y) +
       lbeta(eb_lsoa$y + a, eb_lsoa$n - eb_lsoa$y + b) - lbeta(a, b))
}
eb_fit <- optim(c(log(0.3), log(50)), eb_nll)
eb_a <- exp(eb_fit$par[1]); eb_b <- exp(eb_fit$par[2])
eb_lsoa <- eb_lsoa |>
  mutate(raw = 1000 * y / n, post = 1000 * (y + eb_a) / (n + eb_a + eb_b))

# Horizontal count bar chart for a (factor) survey variable; first level on top.
# True NAs (item-nonresponse) are folded into "Rather not say".
plot_bar <- function(var, base = 12) {
  f <- surv[[var]]
  if (!"Rather not say" %in% levels(f)) levels(f) <- c(levels(f), "Rather not say")
  f[is.na(f)] <- "Rather not say"
  tibble(val = f) |>
    count(val, .drop = FALSE) |>
    filter(n > 0) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = n, y = fct_rev(val))) +
    geom_col(fill = COL_BLUE) +
    geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.25, size = 3) +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = base) +
    theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
}

# --- Kit data: the 500 kit recipients, from the canonical master dataset ---
# golden_envelope (0/1) is the kit-type randomization (the column formerly
# named kit_type). valid_sample is NA for never-returned kits, so returned and
# valid are defined to make never-returned FALSE, not NA. Two nested return
# outcomes: returned = card came back & was lab-assessed; valid = >=1 valid
# blood spot on the card.
master <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/master_dataset.csv",
                   show_col_types = FALSE, name_repair = "minimal")
kitc <- master |>
  filter(!is.na(treatment_kit)) |>
  mutate(
    kit_type = factor(golden_envelope, levels = c(0, 1),
                      labels = c("Kit 0", "Kit 1")),
    exposure = completed_exposure_survey == 1,
    returned = !is.na(DBSLvalidation) | !is.na(DBSRvalidation),
    valid    = !is.na(valid_sample) & valid_sample >= 1,
    disadv = factor(if_else(disadvantaged, "Disadvantaged", "Not disadvantaged"),
                    levels = c("Not disadvantaged", "Disadvantaged")),
    tenure = factor(case_when(
               str_starts(home, "Owned")            ~ "Owned",
               str_detect(home, "private landlord") ~ "Rented (private)",
               str_detect(home, "council")          ~ "Rented (council)"),
             levels = c("Owned", "Rented (private)", "Rented (council)")),
    educ = factor(case_when(
             str_detect(highest_education, "Undergraduate|Postgraduate") ~ "Degree",
             str_detect(highest_education, "age 18|Post-school")         ~ "A-level / vocational",
             str_detect(highest_education, "age 16|No formal")           ~ "<=GCSE / none"),
           levels = c("Degree", "A-level / vocational", "<=GCSE / none")),
    inc = factor(case_when(
            income %in% c("Under £15,000", "£15,000 - £24,999")        ~ "< £25k",
            income %in% c("£25,000 - £34,999", "£35,000 - £49,999")    ~ "£25-50k",
            income %in% c("£50,000 - £74,999", "£75,000 - £99,999")    ~ "£50-100k",
            income %in% c("£100,000 - £149,999", "£150,000 and above") ~ "£100k+"),
          levels = c("£100k+", "£50-100k", "£25-50k", "< £25k")),
    worry = factor(worried_exposure,
             levels = c("Not worried", "A little worried", "Quite worried", "Very worried")),
    knew = factor(prior_lead_knowledge, levels = c("Yes", "No", "Not sure")),
    letter = factor(TREAT_LABELS[treatment_parsed],
                    levels = unname(TREAT_LABELS)))

kits_by_lsoa <- kitc |> count(lsoa, name = "n_kits")

# Per-kit-type counts for the Phase II flowchart (interpolated into the tikz).
kit2 <- kitc |>
  group_by(kit_type) |>
  summarise(n = n(), ret = sum(returned), val = sum(valid),
            exp = sum(exposure), .groups = "drop")

# Snapshot caveat shown on every kit-outcome slide.
kit_caption <- paste0("Counts are a snapshot: kit returns are still arriving, and Round 2 kits ",
                      "(400 of 500) were dispatched later than Round 1's (100).")
```

# Overview

## Recruitment \& Treatment Allocation 

\vspace{1em}
\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    every node/.style={align=center},
    block/.style={draw, rounded corners, fill=blue!8, minimum width=3.2cm,
                  minimum height=2cm, font=\small},
    >={Stealth[length=3mm]}]

  \node (sampling) [block] {\textbf{1. Sampling}\\\textbf{Design}};
  \node (intake) [block, right=1.4cm of sampling] {\textbf{2. Recruitment}\\\textbf{\& Intake Survey}};
  \node (kits) [block, right=1.4cm of intake] {\textbf{3. Kit Allocation,}\\\textbf{\& Exposure Survey}};

  \draw[->, thick] (sampling) -- (intake);
  \draw[->, thick] (intake) -- (kits);
\end{tikzpicture}


## Who responds and why?

- Various kinds of responses: intake survey, exposure survey, return kit, complete experience survey, etc. 
- Design of recruiting materials: randomized controlled trial
- Design of the kits: randomized controlled trial
- Demographic characteristics: observational 



## Description of outcomes

### Started
Opened the baseline survey within 45 days of the letter 

### Usable Response
Eligible child (aged 1--6), valid consent, and valid GP \& contact details provided

### Exposure Survey
Completed survey (preliminary; deadline not yet specified)

### Returned Kit
Blood-spot card came back and was lab-assessed (preliminary; deadline not yet specified)

### Valid Sample
One or more usable blood spot on the returned card

\vspace{0.5em}
\footnotesize Round 1's survey stayed open 7 days past its 45-day window; the 2 responses (1 usable) received in that period are excluded from every count in this deck. One exception, flagged where it appears: the Phase II flowchart shows the realized pool of 890 usable respondents from which kits were allocated.

# 1 - Sampling Design

## Sampling Design: Data Sources

\hypertarget{main-datasources}{}

### Leeds Council Tax Data: 
<!--`leeds_council_tax_data.csv`-->
- Source of residential addresses in Leeds
- 376,205 rows: one per chargeable dwelling
- Columns we use: Address 1-4, Unique Property Reference Number (UPRN)


### ONS address data: 
<!--`ONS_UPRN_NOV_2024_YH.csv`-->
Merge Postcode, OA, LSOA and MSOA with Council Tax data (by UPRN)

### Energy Performance Certificates (EPC): 
<!--`EPC_certificates.csv`-->
Merge Housing age (use latest certificate) with Council Tax data (by UPRN)

### Census Data (2021) at LSOA level 
Merge LSOA-level demographics with Council Tax data (by LSOA)

### Exclusion / De-duplication
\hyperlink{appx-exclusion}{\beamergotobutton{See appendix slide for details}}


## Big differences in HHs with kids across LSOA

```{r}
#| label: lsoa-eligible-share-hist
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 3.2
lsoa_ref |>
  mutate(elig_rate = households_0_9 / total_households) |>
  ggplot(aes(elig_rate)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = COL_BLUE, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Eligible HH in LSOA / Total HH in LSOA", y = "Number of LSOAs") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

From the 2021 Census: households whose youngest dependent child is aged 0--9, one observation per LSOA.


## Stratified Random Sampling to Improve Precision

\alert{How to allocate a total of $N$ letters across LSOAs / housing age?}

### Eligibility
- Letters sent to households without a child aged 1-6 are wasted
- Census proxy: households whose youngest dependent child is aged 0--9 (2021 Census, table RM006)
- $E_\ell =$ (\# such households in LSOA $\ell$) / (Total across Leeds)
- Send $N \times E_\ell$ letters to LSOA $\ell$ (Hamilton allocation)


### Housing Age
- $O_\ell=$ Share of Old houses in LSOA $\ell$
- Suppose $N_\ell$ letters will go to LSOA $\ell$ based on eligibility.
- Send $N_\ell \times O_\ell$ of the $N_\ell$ letters go to Old houses
- The rest go to NonOld houses (Hamilton allocation) 



  

## Where we sent letters

```{r}
#| label: letters-map-and-scatter
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.6
#| fig-height: 4.6
library(patchwork)

# (left) Where letters went: choropleth of letters mailed per LSOA (raw counts).
lsoa_map(letters_by_lsoa, "n_letters", "Letters") +
  theme(legend.key.width = unit(0.3, "cm"))

# (right) Why: letters per LSOA vs estimated eligible share, with marginals.
d   <- letters_by_lsoa |> left_join(lsoa_elig, by = "lsoa")
col <- COL_BLUE

main <- ggplot(d, aes(E_l, n_letters)) +
  geom_abline(slope = sum(d$n_letters), intercept = 0,
              linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.45, colour = col, size = 1.1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(x = "Eligible in LSOA / Total Eligible", y = "Letters mailed") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

top   <- ggplot(d, aes(E_l)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = col, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  theme_void()

right <- ggplot(d, aes(n_letters)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = col, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_void()

top + plot_spacer() + main + right +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2, nrow = 2, widths = c(4, 1), heights = c(1, 4))
```


## Letters sent vs. LSOA deprivation

```{r}
#| label: letters-vs-deprivation
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.8
letters_by_lsoa |>
  left_join(lsoa_imd, by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(share = n_letters / sum(n_letters)) |>
  ggplot(aes(imd_score, share)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.45, colour = COL_BLUE, size = 1.1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, colour = COL_ORANGE,
              fill = COL_ORANGE, alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1)) +
  labs(x = "IMD score  (higher = more deprived)",
       y = "Share of all letters sent",
       caption = "Follows mechanically from the eligibility weighting: letters were allocated proportional to E_l, and families with young children concentrate in more deprived LSOAs.") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```


## Randomized Controlled Trial \#1: Recruiting Letter


### Generalized Risk (G)
> The UK Health Security Agency says that lead can be found in things like: Paint on old walls, doors, windowsills, radiators, furniture, toys, and ornaments; Old plumbing, including pipes, tanks, and taps; Some spices brought from other countries; Some traditional medicines and beauty products (like surma or kohl eye makeup); Certain cooking pots (including grey pewter pots) from countries outside the UK and Europe. 

### Personalized Risk (P)
> We see from local records that your home was built before 1967, so it may have old paint or pipes containing lead. Finding out if your child has come into contact with lead could help protect their health. 

\vspace{1em}
\alert{Control letter contains neither; Personalized Risk letter contains both}


---

![](../img/personalized-risk-letter-2page.png){fig-align="center"}

## Randomization of Letter Types

Recall: Randomization: "personalized" treatment only for Old homes 

- Old stratum: 1/3 control, 1/3 generalized, 1/3 personalized
- NonOld stratum: 1/2 control, 1/2 generalized
  
## Adaptive Design

\alert{Budget for 150k letters and 2k baseline surveys (5 GBP gift card)}

\vspace{1em}

### Round 1  
Send 30k letters; wait 45 days; \# of responses $R_1$ determines letters sent in Round 2 

### Round 2 
- Plug-in response rate $\hat{p} = R_1 / 30{,}000$; send enough to reach 2,000:
  $$\text{send} = \min\!\Big(120\text{k},\ 10\text{k}\times\big\lceil (2000 -
  R_1)\,/\,(10{,}000\,\hat{p})\big\rceil\Big)$$
- Round **up** to next 10k; stop early if $R_1 \geq 2{,}000$ (target met) or $R_1 < 20$ (disaster)

### Realized Allocation

- Based on Round 1 Response Rate: sent 120k letters in Round 2 (150k total)


# 2 - Recruitment \& Intake Survey Results


## Phase 1: Outreach \& Intake Survey

\vspace{1em}

\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.5, every node/.style={align=center}]
  \node (addr) [draw] {All Leeds Residential Addresses \\ $370{,}666$};
  \node (letters) [draw, below of=addr, node distance=2.2cm] {Send `r fmt(n_mailed_total)` Letters};
  \node (started) [draw, below right of=letters, node distance=2.7cm] {`r fmt(n_started)` \\ Started Survey};
  \node (noresp) [draw, dashed, below left of=letters, node distance=2.7cm] {No Response \\ `r fmt(n_mailed_total - n_started)`};
  \node (usable) [draw, below right of=started, node distance=2.7cm] {`r fmt(n_usable)` \\ Usable Responses};
  \node (drop) [draw, dashed, below left of=started, node distance=2.7cm] {Ineligible or \\ no consent \\ `r fmt(n_started - n_usable)`};

  \draw[->] (addr) -- (letters) node[midway, right]{Stratified Random Sample ($\approx 40\%$)};
  \draw[->] (letters) -- (started) node[midway, right]{`r sprintf("%.1f", 100 * n_started / n_mailed_total)`\%};
  \draw[->, dashed] (letters) -- (noresp);
  \draw[->] (started) -- (usable) node[midway, right]{`r sprintf("%.0f", 100 * n_usable / n_started)`\%};
  \draw[->, dashed] (started) -- (drop);
\end{tikzpicture}



## Usable response rates vary with housing age not treatment.

```{r}
#| label: cell-rate-forest
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.9
cell_forest(cell_table, "Usable responses per 1,000 letters mailed")
```

## Highest response from Old (pre 1967); lowest from Unknown (no EPC)

```{r}
#| label: age-status-forest
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.6
age_levels <- c("Old", "New", "Unknown")

age_tab <- addr |>
  count(age_grp, treatment, name = "mailed") |>
  left_join(
    raw |>
      filter(usable) |>
      mutate(age_grp = age_grp_from_status(age_status)) |>
      count(age_grp, treatment = treatment_parsed, name = "usable"),
    by = c("age_grp", "treatment")
  ) |>
  add_score_ci(usable, mailed) |>
  mutate(age_grp = factor(age_grp, levels = rev(age_levels)),
         treatment = factor(TREAT_LABELS[treatment],
                            levels = unname(TREAT_LABELS)))

ggplot(age_tab, aes(x = est, y = age_grp, colour = treatment)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2.5, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi),
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), height = 0.2, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 2.8) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = TREAT_COLS) +
  labs(x = "Usable responses per 1,000 letters mailed", y = NULL, colour = NULL,
       caption = paste0("dashed line = design Old/Non-Old cut; ", ci_caption)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```



## Treatment affects *starting* the survey.


```{r}
#| label: click-rate-forest
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.4
cell_table |>
  select(cell, n_responded, n_mailed) |>
  add_score_ci(n_responded, n_mailed) |>
  cell_forest("Any response (clicked / started) per 1,000 letters mailed")
```


## Treatment effects: starting vs. usable response

```{r}
#| label: treatment-diff-forest
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.4
# Differences in response rates between randomized letter arms, for both
# outcomes: started (clicked) and usable. Newcombe score intervals (score_diff
# in setup); returns proportions, scaled to per-1,000 below.
contrast_levels <- c("Non-Old:  G − C", "Old:  G − C", "Old:  P − C",
                     "Old:  P − G", "Old:  (G + P) − C")

contrast_tbl <- function(outcome_col) {
  v <- cell_table |>
    transmute(cell = as.character(cell), x = .data[[outcome_col]], n = n_mailed)
  g <- function(c, col) v[[col]][v$cell == c]
  tribble(
    ~contrast,            ~x1,                            ~n1,                            ~x0,                  ~n0,
    "Non-Old:  G − C",    g("NonOld_G", "x"),             g("NonOld_G", "n"),             g("NonOld_C", "x"),   g("NonOld_C", "n"),
    "Old:  G − C",        g("Old_G", "x"),                g("Old_G", "n"),                g("Old_C", "x"),      g("Old_C", "n"),
    "Old:  P − C",        g("Old_P", "x"),                g("Old_P", "n"),                g("Old_C", "x"),      g("Old_C", "n"),
    "Old:  P − G",        g("Old_P", "x"),                g("Old_P", "n"),                g("Old_G", "x"),      g("Old_G", "n"),
    "Old:  (G + P) − C",  g("Old_G", "x") + g("Old_P", "x"), g("Old_G", "n") + g("Old_P", "n"), g("Old_C", "x"), g("Old_C", "n"))
}

bind_rows(
  contrast_tbl("n_responded") |> mutate(outcome = "Started (clicked)"),
  contrast_tbl("n_usable")    |> mutate(outcome = "Usable response")) |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(d = list(score_diff(x1, n1, x0, n0))) |>
  ungroup() |>
  unnest(d) |>
  mutate(across(c(diff, lo, hi), \(x) 1000 * x),
         contrast = factor(contrast, levels = rev(contrast_levels)),
         outcome  = factor(outcome, levels = c("Started (clicked)",
                                               "Usable response"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = diff, y = contrast)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi), height = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8,
                 colour = COL_BLUE) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = COL_BLUE) +
  facet_wrap(~ outcome) +
  labs(x = "Difference per 1,000 letters mailed", y = NULL,
       caption = paste0(ci_pct, " Newcombe score intervals. ",
                        "(G + P) pools the arms sharing the generalized-risk text: the P letter contains both messages.")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```



## Survey drop-off: the consent page is the main exit

```{r}
#| label: dropoff-funnel
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.3
# Item 6 (Ludovica's request). Classes come from the funnel flags (eligible,
# valid_consent, gp_provided, Finished), which the pseudonymized files retain
# for non-usable rows; the raw survey answers are blanked at pseudonymization.
# "Stopped at the consent page": reported an eligible child but no valid
# consent -- all such rows sit at Progress == 32, identifying that value as
# the consent page. "No eligible child": finished (Progress 100) but
# classified ineligible, i.e., screened out at the eligibility question.
DROP_CLS <- c("Completed, usable", "Stopped at the consent page",
              "Left in the first pages", "No eligible child (screened out)",
              "Consented, no GP details")
DROP_COLS <- c("Completed, usable" = COL_BLUE,
               "Stopped at the consent page" = COL_ORANGE,
               "Left in the first pages" = "grey55",
               "No eligible child (screened out)" = "grey40",
               "Consented, no GP details" = "grey70")
dropoff <- raw |>
  mutate(cls = case_when(
    usable ~ "Completed, usable",
    eligible & !valid_consent ~ "Stopped at the consent page",
    eligible & valid_consent & !gp_provided ~ "Consented, no GP details",
    !eligible & Finished ~ "No eligible child (screened out)",
    TRUE ~ "Left in the first pages"),
    cls = factor(cls, levels = DROP_CLS))

dropoff |>
  count(cls) |>
  mutate(share = n / sum(n),
         lab = paste0(n, "  (", sprintf("%.0f", 100 * share), "%)")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = n, y = fct_rev(cls), fill = cls)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = lab), hjust = -0.08, size = 3.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = DROP_COLS) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +
  labs(x = "Started responses (45-day window)", y = NULL,
       caption = paste0("All ", nrow(dropoff), " started responses (45-day window); classes from the funnel flags, which are retained for non-usable rows.\n",
                        "Of the 1,269 households reporting an eligible child, ",
                        sum(dropoff$cls == "Stopped at the consent page"),
                        " (29%) stopped exactly at the consent page, after the risk message.")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Survey drop-off: where leavers stop

```{r}
#| label: dropoff-pages
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.3
# Progress-at-exit for everyone who started but did not end usable or
# screened-out. Page identities are inferred from the funnel flags of those
# who stop there: every consent-incomplete row sits at Progress 32 (the
# consent page); GP-missing rows sit at 62/68; values below 32 precede the
# eligibility question. The c/g/p page timers (one per treatment arm) show
# whether a leaver submitted their arm's risk-message page.
leavers <- dropoff |>
  filter(!cls %in% c("Completed, usable", "No eligible child (screened out)")) |>
  mutate(passed_msg = !is.na(coalesce(`c_timer_Page Submit`,
                                      `g_timer_Page Submit`,
                                      `p_timer_Page Submit`)))
early_share <- leavers |>
  filter(cls == "Left in the first pages") |>
  summarise(m = mean(!passed_msg)) |>
  pull(m)

leavers |>
  count(cls, Progress) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(Progress), y = n, fill = cls)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  annotate("text", x = factor(32), y = 364, label = "consent page",
           vjust = -0.6, size = 3.4, colour = "grey25") +
  annotate("text", x = factor(62), y = 40, label = "GP details",
           hjust = 0.2, vjust = -0.6, size = 3.4, colour = "grey25") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = DROP_COLS, name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  labs(x = "Qualtrics Progress (%) when the session ended", y = "Responses",
       caption = paste0("The ", nrow(leavers), " started responses that ended before completion (screened-out ineligibles excluded). ",
                        "Page labels inferred from the funnel flags of those who stop there.\n",
                        sprintf("%.0f", 100 * early_share),
                        "% of the first-pages leavers never submitted their arm's risk-message page (its page timer is empty).")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Survey drop-off: when people start, relative to mailing

```{r}
#| label: dropoff-timing
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.3
# Days from the round's mailing date to StartDate, by round and by whether
# the response ended usable. Both rounds' responses cluster in the first two
# weeks; the 45-day window is the outcome definition, not a binding deadline.
timing <- raw |>
  mutate(mail_date = if_else(round == 1, as.Date("2025-11-14"), as.Date("2026-01-22")),
         days = as.numeric(as.Date(StartDate) - mail_date),
         round_lab = paste("Round", round),
         outcome = if_else(usable, "Usable", "Not usable"))
med <- timing |> group_by(round_lab) |> summarise(m = median(days))

ggplot(timing, aes(days, colour = round_lab, linetype = outcome)) +
  stat_ecdf(linewidth = 0.7, pad = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Round 1" = COL_ORANGE, "Round 2" = COL_BLUE),
                      name = NULL) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Usable" = "solid", "Not usable" = "42"),
                        name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 45, 7), limits = c(0, 45)) +
  labs(x = "Days from mailing to starting the survey",
       y = "Share of responses started",
       caption = paste0("All started responses, 45-day window. Median days to start: ",
                        "Round 1 ", med$m[1], ", Round 2 ", med$m[2], ".")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Intake Survey: Awareness of Lead

\small Usable respondents ($N = `r nrow(surv)`$); counts shown on bars.

```{r}
#| label: lead-awareness
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.6
#| fig-height: 3.4
plot_bar("prior_lead_knowledge") + ggtitle("Heard of lead risk before?")
plot_bar("worried_exposure") + ggtitle("Worried about exposure?")
```

## Intake Survey: Socioeconomic Profile


```{r}
#| label: ses-profile
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.9
#| fig-height: 3.6
plot_bar("income") + ggtitle("Household income")
plot_bar("highest_education") + ggtitle("Highest education")
```

## Intake Survey: Housing \& Ethnicity

```{r}
#| label: housing-ethnicity
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.9
#| fig-height: 3.6
plot_bar("home") + ggtitle("Housing tenure")
plot_bar("ethnicity") + ggtitle("Ethnicity")
```


## Lower response rate in more deprived LSOAs

\hypertarget{main-deprivation}{}

```{r}
#| label: usable-imd-marginal
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.3
imd_summary(imd_cells) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = rate, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(colour = COL_BLUE, linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), colour = COL_BLUE, size = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "Usable per 1,000", caption = ci_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"),
        plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 8, b = 4, l = 6))
```

\footnotesize Background:
\hyperlink{appx-imd}{\beamergotobutton{What is IMD?}}
\hyperlink{appx-imd-dist}{\beamergotobutton{England vs Leeds}}
\hyperlink{appx-imd-map}{\beamergotobutton{Leeds deprivation map}}
\hyperlink{appx-eb}{\beamergotobutton{Per-LSOA maps \& shrinkage}}

## ... but mainly driven by Old houses

\hypertarget{main-by-age}{}

```{r}
#| label: usable-imd-by-age
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.3
imd_summary(imd_cells, "age_grp", outcome = "usable") |>
  mutate(age_grp = factor(age_grp, levels = c("Old", "New", "Unknown"))) |>
  gradient_plot("age_grp", AGE_COLS, "Usable per 1,000")
```

\footnotesize \hyperlink{appx-imd-treat}{\beamergotobutton{Does the gradient differ by treatment?}}
\hyperlink{appx-click-age}{\beamergotobutton{Clicks version of this plot}}

## More Old housing in deprived LSOAs

```{r}
#| label: stock-composition
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.6
addr_imd |>
  mutate(q = bin_imd(imd_score)) |>
  filter(!is.na(q)) |>
  count(q, age_grp) |>
  mutate(age_grp = factor(age_grp, levels = c("Old", "New", "Unknown"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = n, fill = age_grp)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill", width = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Old" = COL_ORANGE, "New" = COL_BLUE,
                               "Unknown" = "#7A7A7A")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent()) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "Share of letters mailed", fill = NULL,
       caption = "Within each LSOA, letters were split across housing ages in proportion to the address stock, so this is also the stock's composition.") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Responder demographics versus LSOA deprivation

```{r}
#| label: responder-traits-imd
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.9
ind_levels <- c("Low income (≤£25k)", "Renting", "Low education", "Non-White")

raw |>
  filter(usable) |>
  left_join(lsoa_imd, by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(
    q = bin_imd(imd_score),
    `Low income (≤£25k)` = income %in% c("Under £15,000", "£15,000 - £24,999"),
    `Renting`            = home %in% c("Rented from the council",
                                       "Rented from a private landlord"),
    `Low education`      = highest_education %in%
      c("No formal qualifications", "Education up to age 16 (GCSE or equivalent)"),
    `Non-White`          = !is.na(ethnicity) & !str_starts(ethnicity, "White") &
                             ethnicity != "Rather not say"
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(q)) |>
  select(q, all_of(ind_levels)) |>
  pivot_longer(-q, names_to = "indicator", values_to = "flag") |>
  group_by(indicator, q) |>
  summarise(x = sum(flag, na.rm = TRUE), n = sum(!is.na(flag)), .groups = "drop") |>
  add_score_ci(x, n, scale = 100) |>
  mutate(indicator = factor(indicator, levels = ind_levels)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = q, y = est, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(colour = COL_BLUE, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), colour = COL_BLUE, size = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ indicator, nrow = 1) +
  labs(x = "IMD quintile  (left = least deprived)",
       y = "% of responders",
       caption = paste0(ci_caption,
                        ". Item non-response ('Rather not say' / blank) counts toward denominators, not numerators.")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```

```{r}
#| label: elig-share-prep
#| include: false
# Items 1 / 2 / 2b (Ludovica's requests). One row per LSOA (488): letters
# mailed, started / usable / eligible-flagged responses (45-day window), the
# census eligible share (households whose youngest child is 0-9, RM006), and
# the IMD quintile. WLS with letters weights; robust (HC1) SEs for captions.
lsoa_elig_tab <- addr |>
  count(lsoa, name = "letters") |>
  left_join(raw |> group_by(lsoa) |>
              summarise(started = n(), usable = sum(usable),
                        elig = sum(eligible),
                        answered = sum(eligible | Finished), .groups = "drop"),
            by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(across(c(started, usable, elig, answered), \(x) replace_na(x, 0L))) |>
  left_join(lsoa_ref |> transmute(lsoa = lsoa21cd,
                                  share = households_0_9 / total_households),
            by = "lsoa") |>
  left_join(lsoa_imd, by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(q_lab = factor(gsub("\n", " ", as.character(bin_imd(imd_score))),
                        levels = gsub("\n", " ", levels(bin_imd(imd_score)))),
         rate = 1000 * usable / letters,
         per_elig = 1000 * usable / (share * letters))

m_share <- lm(rate ~ share, data = lsoa_elig_tab, weights = letters)
se_share <- sqrt(sandwich::vcovHC(m_share, type = "HC1")[2, 2])
m_pere <- lm(per_elig ~ share, data = lsoa_elig_tab,
             weights = share * letters)
se_pere <- sqrt(sandwich::vcovHC(m_pere, type = "HC1")[2, 2])
```

## Response vs. eligibility is a deprivation story

\small

### What the next four slides show
- We mailed where the census says young children live, but responses barely followed: 3$\times$ the eligible share brings $\approx$ 20% more responses per letter, not 200%
- It is not a map problem: a current ONS map of the exact eligible birth cohort predicts response identically to the 2021 census share
- It is a deprivation problem: eligible families cluster in deprived areas, and deprived areas do not respond. Where deprivation is low, response tracks eligibility roughly one-for-one; in the most deprived fifth of Leeds it does not track it at all
- And it is eligible families *not responding*, not letters missing them: 92% of those who answer the survey's first question report an eligible child, everywhere in Leeds

### Two consequences
- Design: eligibility targeting yielded about 8% more usable responses than mailing the same 150,000 letters uniformly would have (realized 889 vs. $\approx$ 820, applying each LSOA's realized rate)
- Phase II: the sample under-represents eligible children in deprived areas, where lead risk is highest

## Usable response rises only weakly with the LSOA's eligible share

```{r}
#| label: elig-share-scatter
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.5
ggplot(lsoa_elig_tab, aes(share, rate)) +
  geom_smooth(aes(weight = letters), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
              colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.7, fill = "grey80") +
  geom_point(aes(size = letters, fill = q_lab), shape = 21,
             colour = "grey35", stroke = 0.2, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlOrBr", name = "IMD quintile") +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 4, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 20)) +
  labs(x = "Census share of households with a young child (LSOA)",
       y = "Usable per 1,000 letters",
       caption = sprintf(paste0("One dot per LSOA (488); dot area = letters mailed; %d dots above 20 per 1,000 are outside the plot area but in the fit.\n",
                        "Line: WLS (letters weights); slope +%.1f usable per 1,000 letters per +10 pp of eligible share (robust SE %.1f).\n",
                        "High-share LSOAs skew deprived (Q4-Q5), where response is lower --- the two gradients offset."),
                        sum(lsoa_elig_tab$rate > 20),
                        coef(m_share)[2] / 10, se_share / 10)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

## A fresher eligibility map would not have helped

```{r}
#| label: elig-map-test
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.4
# Does the near-flat gradient reflect a stale census map? Test: replace the
# 2021 census household share with ONS mid-year child counts per frame
# address (ECLIPS-PAP Script 29): children aged 0-5 at mid-2024 track the
# eligible birth cohort ("best now"; first published 2025-11-07, three weeks
# AFTER the design froze on 2025-10-14), children 0-3 at mid-2022 are the
# newest vintage knowable at planning time. Each slope is shown as a % of its
# own proportional benchmark (100% = usable responses scale one-for-one with
# the measure; benchmark = overall rate / letter-weighted mean of the
# measure). "Holding deprivation fixed" adds a cubic in imd_score.
kids <- read_csv("~/ECLIPS-PAP/data/processed/lsoa-children-estimates.csv",
                 show_col_types = FALSE)
mt <- lsoa_elig_tab |>
  left_join(kids, by = "lsoa") |>
  left_join(lsoa_ref |> select(lsoa = lsoa21cd, n_addresses), by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(`Census 2021: households with young child` = share,
         `Mid-2024 estimates: children 0-5 per address` = kids_0_5_mid2024 / n_addresses,
         `Mid-2022 estimates: children 0-3 per address` = kids_0_3_mid2022 / n_addresses)
MEASURES <- c("Census 2021: households with young child",
              "Mid-2024 estimates: children 0-5 per address",
              "Mid-2022 estimates: children 0-3 per address")

fit_pct <- function(x, hold_imd) {
  d <- mt |> mutate(x = x)
  bench <- (sum(d$usable) / sum(d$letters)) * 1000 / weighted.mean(d$x, d$letters)
  f <- if (hold_imd) rate ~ x + poly(imd_score, 3) else rate ~ x
  m <- lm(f, data = d, weights = letters)
  se <- sqrt(sandwich::vcovHC(m, type = "HC1")[2, 2])
  tibble(pct = 100 * coef(m)[2] / bench,
         lo  = 100 * (coef(m)[2] - 1.96 * se) / bench,
         hi  = 100 * (coef(m)[2] + 1.96 * se) / bench)
}
slopes <- crossing(measure = MEASURES,
                   spec = c("Raw", "Holding deprivation fixed")) |>
  mutate(res = map2(measure, spec,
                    \(mm, ss) fit_pct(mt[[mm]], ss == "Holding deprivation fixed"))) |>
  unnest(res)

slopes |>
  mutate(measure = factor(measure, levels = rev(MEASURES)),
         spec = factor(spec, levels = c("Raw", "Holding deprivation fixed"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(pct, measure, colour = spec)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 100, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey40") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi), height = 0.18, linewidth = 0.7,
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.6, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  annotate("text", x = 100, y = 0.55, label = "proportional ",
           hjust = 1, size = 3, colour = "grey30") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Raw" = "grey45",
                                 "Holding deprivation fixed" = COL_BLUE),
                      name = NULL) +
  labs(x = "Response-vs-eligibility slope, as % of proportional (95% CI)",
       y = NULL,
       caption = paste0("100% = usable responses scale one-for-one with the measure (benchmark: overall rate / letter-weighted mean); robust SEs.\n",
                        "The mid-2024 measure targets the eligible birth cohort and correlates 0.85 with the census share; it predicts response no\n",
                        "better, and in the most deprived quintile both slopes are ~0. The mid-2023/24 estimates were first published 2025-11-07,\n",
                        "after the 2025-10-14 design freeze, so no fresher map existed at planning time. ONS mid-year estimates; ECLIPS-PAP Script 29.")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Response per (census-)eligible household falls as the eligible share rises

```{r}
#| label: per-elig-scatter
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.5
# Item 2: same numerator, denominator = census share x letters, under the
# stated assumption Pr(eligible | mailed, LSOA) = census share (the
# back-of-envelope per-eligible estimand of phase1-analysis-plan.md Part 2).
ggplot(lsoa_elig_tab, aes(share, per_elig)) +
  geom_smooth(aes(weight = share * letters), method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
              colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.7, fill = "grey80") +
  geom_point(aes(size = share * letters, fill = q_lab), shape = 21,
             colour = "grey35", stroke = 0.2, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlOrBr", name = "IMD quintile") +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 4, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(x = "Census share of households with a young child (LSOA)",
       y = "Per 1,000 eligible mailings",
       caption = sprintf(paste0("Denominator: census share x letters mailed; assumes Pr(eligible | mailed) equals the census share in each LSOA.\n",
                        "Dot area = estimated eligible mailings; %d dots above 100 are outside the plot area but in the fit.\n",
                        "Line: WLS (eligible-mailing weights); slope %.0f per 1,000 per +10 pp (robust SE %.0f).\n",
                        "No adjustment for flagged non-household buildings (building-evidence.csv), which inflate a few LSOAs' letter counts.\n",
                        "Arithmetic companion to the raw-rate slide: dividing its nearly flat rate by the rising share yields this decline by construction."),
                        sum(lsoa_elig_tab$per_elig > 100),
                        coef(m_pere)[2] / 10, se_pere / 10)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Those who answer the eligibility question say yes $\approx$ 92% of the time, everywhere

```{r}
#| label: elig-vs-census
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.5
# Item 2b, recut 2026-07-12: denominator = starters who ANSWERED the
# eligibility question (reported an eligible child -> `eligible`, or reported
# none and were screened out -> Finished & !eligible). The first version used
# all started responses, which mixed "answered no" with "left before the
# question" -- and early leaving rises with deprivation, so its gradient
# partly reflected drop-off (see the drop-off slides), not composition.
# Conditions on responding: eligible households respond at far higher rates,
# so the level sits far above the census share by construction; the
# informative part is the (near-absent) gradient.
l2b <- lsoa_elig_tab |> filter(answered > 0)
bins_2b <- l2b |>
  arrange(share) |>
  mutate(b = ntile(cumsum(answered), 10)) |>
  group_by(b) |>
  summarise(share = weighted.mean(share, answered),
            elig = sum(elig), answered = sum(answered), .groups = "drop") |>
  add_score_ci(elig, answered, scale = 100)

ggplot(bins_2b, aes(share, est)) +
  geom_point(data = l2b, aes(share, 100 * elig / answered, size = answered),
             colour = "grey75", alpha = 0.6, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 100 * sum(l2b$elig) / sum(l2b$answered),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi), colour = COL_BLUE, size = 0.45) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 3.5, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(x = "Census share of households with a young child (LSOA)",
       y = "% answering yes",
       caption = paste0("Answered = reported an eligible child (1,269) or reported none and was screened out (107); the 246 who left before\n",
                        "the question are excluded (their share rises with deprivation --- see the drop-off slides).\n",
                        "Blue: deciles by census share (weighted by answers), 95% Wilson intervals; grey: LSOAs (area = answers); dashed: overall 92%.\n",
                        "Conditions on responding: eligible households respond at far higher rates, so the level is high by construction;\n",
                        "the census share barely predicts the eligibility of those who answer.")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

# 3 - Kit Allocation and Exposure Survey

## Kit Allocation

\footnotesize

### Flag as "disadvantaged" if *any* of the following:
- Low income: $\leq$ £24,999
- Low education:  "No formal qualifications" or "Education up to age 16"
- Language: English non-native *and* reading/writing "Quite hard" / "Very hard"

### Re-balancing
- Budgeted for 500 kits and associated lab capacity
- Expect fewer responses from some groups, so recruit more participants than kits
- Earmark 50\% of kits for "disadvantaged"; choose randomly among them
- Allocate remaining kits randomly among not disadvantaged

### Randomize Kit Types
- 50-50 random assignment *stratified* by (Letter treatment) $\times$ (Disadvantaged)

### Across rounds (realized)
- Split 500 kits across rounds $\propto$ expected responses; selection probabilities round-specific
- All *kit-eligible* disadvantaged got a kit: 100 kits / 49 disadvantaged (R1); 400 kits / 168 of 176 (R2)
<!-- The 8 R2 disadvantaged non-recipients were excluded at the Script 15/16
     review gate: 6 could not provide a verifiable GP, 2 had bounced emails.
     Confirmed by Andrea in #general 2026-06-09 (thread ts 1781003461.772679)
     and recorded per participant in master_dataset.csv kit_review_note. -->

## Where we sent kits

```{r}
#| label: kits-map-and-deprivation
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4.6
#| fig-height: 4.6
# (left) Where kits went: choropleth of kits sent per LSOA (kits_by_lsoa from setup).
lsoa_map(kits_by_lsoa, "n_kits", "Kits") +
  theme(legend.key.width = unit(0.3, "cm"))

# (right) Kits sent per LSOA vs deprivation (all 488 LSOAs; 0 if none).
lsoa_imd |>
  left_join(kits_by_lsoa, by = "lsoa") |>
  mutate(n_kits = replace_na(n_kits, 0L)) |>
  ggplot(aes(imd_score, n_kits)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0, height = 0.18, alpha = 0.4, colour = COL_BLUE,
              size = 1.1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, colour = COL_ORANGE,
              fill = COL_ORANGE, alpha = 0.15, linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(x = "IMD score  (higher = more deprived)", y = "Kits sent in LSOA") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

## Phase II: Collecting Blood Samples

\centering
\resizebox{0.82\textwidth}{!}{%
\begin{tikzpicture}[every node/.style={draw, align=center, font=\small}, >=stealth]
  \node (usable) at (0,0)     {`r sum(master$usable)` Usable Respondents};
  \node (kits)   at (0,-1.6)  {Send `r nrow(kitc)` Kits + Exposure Survey};
  \node (kitA)   at (-3.3,-3.4) {Kit A \\ `r kit2$n[1]`};
  \node (kitB)   at (3.3,-3.4)  {Kit B \\ `r kit2$n[2]`};
  \node (Aret)   at (-5,-5.7)  {Returned kit \\ `r kit2$ret[1]` (`r round(100 * kit2$ret[1] / kit2$n[1])`\%) \\ {\scriptsize `r kit2$val[1]` valid}};
  \node (Asur)   at (-1.7,-5.7) {Exposure survey \\ `r kit2$exp[1]` (`r round(100 * kit2$exp[1] / kit2$n[1])`\%)};
  \node (Bret)   at (1.7,-5.7)  {Returned kit \\ `r kit2$ret[2]` (`r round(100 * kit2$ret[2] / kit2$n[2])`\%) \\ {\scriptsize `r kit2$val[2]` valid}};
  \node (Bsur)   at (5,-5.7)   {Exposure survey \\ `r kit2$exp[2]` (`r round(100 * kit2$exp[2] / kit2$n[2])`\%)};

  \draw[->] (usable) -- (kits) node[midway, right, draw=none]{re-balanced $\approx `r round(100 * nrow(kitc) / sum(master$usable))`$\%};
  \draw[->] (kits) -- (kitA);
  \draw[->] (kits) -- (kitB) node[midway, above right, draw=none]{randomize kit type};
  \draw[->] (kitA) -- (Aret);
  \draw[->] (kitA) -- (Asur);
  \draw[->] (kitB) -- (Bret);
  \draw[->] (kitB) -- (Bsur);
\end{tikzpicture}%
}

\footnotesize Kits were allocated from the realized usable pool (`r sum(master$usable)`, one more than the 45-day-outcome count used elsewhere in this deck). `r kit_caption`

## No Effect of Kit Type \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-type-forest
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.6
# Among the 500 kit recipients (kitc from setup; outcomes defined there).
outcomes <- c("Completed exposure survey" = "exposure",
              "Returned a kit (any)"      = "returned",
              "Returned >=1 valid spot"   = "valid")

kitc |>
  pivot_longer(all_of(unname(outcomes)), names_to = "outcome", values_to = "hit") |>
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome, levels = rev(unname(outcomes)),
                          labels = rev(names(outcomes)))) |>
  group_by(kit_type, outcome) |>
  summarise(x = sum(hit), n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  add_score_ci(x, n, scale = 100) |>
  ggplot(aes(est, outcome, colour = kit_type)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi),
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.55), fatten = 2.5) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1),
                     limits = c(0, NA)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(COL_BLUE, COL_ORANGE2), name = NULL) +
  labs(x = paste0("Share of ", nrow(kitc), " kit recipients (", ci_pct, " Wilson CI)"),
       y = NULL, caption = kit_caption) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "top",
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```


## Returned kit vs. completed exposure survey \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

\vspace{1em}

```{r}
#| label: kit-exposure-confusion
#| results: asis
# 2x2 of the two Phase II outcomes among all 500 kit recipients.
cm <- table(
  factor(if_else(kitc$returned, "Kit returned", "Kit not returned"),
         levels = c("Kit returned", "Kit not returned")),
  factor(if_else(kitc$exposure, "Survey completed", "Survey not completed"),
         levels = c("Survey completed", "Survey not completed"))
) |>
  addmargins(FUN = list(Total = sum), quiet = TRUE)

knitr::kable(cm, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE,
             col.names = c("Survey completed", "Survey not completed", "Total"))
```

\vspace{1em}
\footnotesize Counts of the `r nrow(kitc)` kit recipients. `r kit_caption`

```{r}
#| label: kit-covariate-prep
#| include: false
# Covariate descriptives for the 500 kit recipients (kitc built in setup).
# Rate + Wilson CI (in %) for one categorical var, both return outcomes; drop tiny cats.
kit_forest <- function(var, min_n = 10) {
  kitc |>
    filter(!is.na(.data[[var]])) |>
    pivot_longer(c(returned, valid), names_to = "outcome", values_to = "hit") |>
    mutate(outcome = factor(outcome, levels = c("returned", "valid"),
                            labels = c("Returned any kit", ">=1 valid spot"))) |>
    group_by(level = .data[[var]], outcome) |>
    summarise(x = sum(hit), n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
    filter(n >= min_n) |>
    add_score_ci(x, n, scale = 100)
}

plot_kit_forest <- function(df) {
  ggplot(df, aes(est, fct_rev(level), colour = outcome)) +
    geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi),
                    position = position_dodge(width = 0.55), fatten = 2.2) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1), limits = c(0, NA)) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c(COL_BLUE, COL_ORANGE2), name = NULL) +
    labs(x = paste0("Share of kit recipients (", ci_pct, " Wilson CI)"), y = NULL,
         caption = kit_caption) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "top",
          plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
}
```

## Disadvantaged participants less likely to return kits \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-disadvantaged
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 2.4
plot_kit_forest(kit_forest("disadv"))
```

## Disadvantaged participants less likely to return kits \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-disadv-diff
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 2.6
# Newcombe interval (score_diff in setup); contrast = disadvantaged - not.
outc <- c("Returned any kit" = "returned", ">=1 valid spot" = "valid")
imap_dfr(outc, \(col, lab) {
  d1 <- filter(kitc, disadv == "Disadvantaged")
  d0 <- filter(kitc, disadv == "Not disadvantaged")
  score_diff(sum(d1[[col]]), nrow(d1), sum(d0[[col]]), nrow(d0)) |>
    mutate(outcome = lab)
}) |>
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome, levels = rev(names(outc))),
         across(c(diff, lo, hi), \(x) 100 * x)) |>
  ggplot(aes(diff, outcome)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi), colour = COL_BLUE,
                  fatten = 3, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Disadvantaged - not disadvantaged (percentage points)", y = NULL,
       caption = paste0(ci_pct, " Newcombe score interval")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, colour = "grey40"))
```


## Valid spot given return, by disadvantage \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-valid-given-return
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 2.4
# Among returned kits only: share with >=1 valid blood spot, by disadvantage.
# (The earlier slides use all 500 recipients, conflating return and validity.)
vr <- kitc |>
  filter(returned) |>
  group_by(disadv) |>
  summarise(x = sum(valid), n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  add_score_ci(x, n, scale = 100)

vr_diff <- score_diff(
  vr$x[vr$disadv == "Disadvantaged"],     vr$n[vr$disadv == "Disadvantaged"],
  vr$x[vr$disadv == "Not disadvantaged"], vr$n[vr$disadv == "Not disadvantaged"])

vr |>
  mutate(label = paste0(disadv, "  (", x, " of ", n, ")")) |>
  ggplot(aes(est, fct_rev(label))) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi), colour = COL_BLUE,
                  fatten = 3, linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1),
                     limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(x = paste0("Share of returned kits with >=1 valid spot (", ci_pct,
                  " Wilson CI)"),
       y = NULL,
       caption = paste0(
         "Difference (disadvantaged - not): ",
         sprintf("%.1f", 100 * vr_diff$diff), " pp [",
         sprintf("%.1f", 100 * vr_diff$lo), ", ",
         sprintf("%.1f", 100 * vr_diff$hi), "], ", ci_pct,
         " Newcombe interval. ", kit_caption)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Housing Tenure and Education Predict Return Rates \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-ses
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 4.1
bind_rows(
  kit_forest("tenure") |> mutate(panel = "Housing tenure"),
  kit_forest("educ")   |> mutate(panel = "Education")) |>
  mutate(level = factor(level, levels = c("Owned", "Rented (private)", "Rented (council)",
                                          "Degree", "A-level / vocational", "<=GCSE / none")),
         panel = factor(panel, levels = c("Housing tenure", "Education"))) |>
  plot_kit_forest() +
  facet_grid(panel ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y")
```



## Income and Kit Return Rates \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-income
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.0
plot_kit_forest(kit_forest("inc"))
```

## Worry / Prior Lead Knowledge and Return Rates \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-attitudes
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 4.1
bind_rows(
  kit_forest("worry") |> mutate(panel = "Worried about lead?"),
  kit_forest("knew")  |> mutate(panel = "Knew about lead beforehand?")) |>
  mutate(level = factor(level, levels = c("Not worried", "A little worried",
                                          "Quite worried", "Very worried",
                                          "Yes", "No", "Not sure")),
         panel = factor(panel, levels = c("Worried about lead?",
                                          "Knew about lead beforehand?"))) |>
  plot_kit_forest() +
  facet_grid(panel ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  labs(caption = paste0(
    sum(kitc$worried_exposure %in% c("Not sure", "Rather not say")),
    " recipients answering 'Not sure' / 'Rather not say' on worry are omitted from the top panel. ",
    kit_caption))
```



<!--## Feedback survey
 
- It was opened a few days ago we think? Ask Andrea
- Perhaps we could present some very preliminary results?-->


## Timing of kit returns \hfill \small \alert{(Preliminary)}

```{r}
#| label: kit-return-cdf
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.4
# Cumulative kit returns as a share of each round's dispatched kits, by lab
# receipt date, calendar time (date_posted is empty in the lab file, so
# days-since-dispatch is not computable per kit). Each curve levels off at the
# round's return rate to date -- the input to the window-close decision
# (ANALYSIS-TODO item 10). One panel per round, because the rounds differ in
# data quality, not just timing: Round 2's returns are fully dated, but 20 of
# Round 1's 33 returns lack date_received, so its curve is a LOWER BOUND drawn
# from 13 dated returns -- stated inside the panel, not just the caption, so
# the omission Ludovica would otherwise have to infer is surfaced. Open
# circles: total returned to date including undated returns. Earlier versions
# plotted the ECDF of dated returns (ends at 100% by construction, hiding the
# level), then Round 2 alone (hid Round 1's data problem); reworked
# 2026-07-11. Data flags raised with Andrea in #general 2026-07-10 (ts
# 1783723563.956829; no reply as of 2026-07-11): 20 of the 154 returned kits
# lack date_received; 3 rows have date_sampled after date_received; one
# date_sampled reads 2026-09-03 (a future date).
sent_by_round <- kitc |> count(round, name = "sent")
ret_dated <- kitc |>
  filter(returned, !is.na(date_received)) |>
  mutate(received = dmy(date_received),
         round_lab = paste("Round", round))

last_receipt <- max(ret_dated$received)

cum <- ret_dated |>
  arrange(received) |>
  group_by(round, round_lab) |>
  mutate(share = row_number()) |>
  ungroup() |>
  left_join(sent_by_round, by = "round") |>
  mutate(share = share / sent)

ends <- cum |>
  group_by(round, round_lab) |>
  slice_max(received, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(received = last_receipt)

tot_marks <- kitc |>
  group_by(round) |>
  summarise(share = sum(returned) / n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(round_lab = paste("Round", round), received = last_receipt)

r1_note <- tibble(
  round_lab = "Round 1",
  received = min(cum$received), share = 0.31,
  label = paste0("only ", sum(ret_dated$round == 1), " of ",
                 sum(kitc$returned & kitc$round == 1),
                 " returns have receipt dates:\ncurve = lower bound (missing dates flagged\nwith Warwick 2026-07-10, no reply yet)"))

ggplot(bind_rows(cum, ends), aes(received, share, colour = round_lab)) +
  geom_step(linewidth = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = tot_marks, shape = 21, size = 2.6, stroke = 0.9,
             fill = "white", show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(data = r1_note, aes(label = label), hjust = 0, vjust = 1,
            size = 2.9, colour = "grey25", lineheight = 1.1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = last_receipt, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey55") +
  facet_wrap(~ round_lab) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(),
                     limits = c(0, 0.37)) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month", date_labels = "%b %Y",
               expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.06))) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Round 1" = COL_ORANGE, "Round 2" = COL_BLUE)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Share of round's kits returned",
       caption = paste0("Curves: returns with a lab receipt date, as a share of kits dispatched (Round 1: ",
                        sent_by_round$sent[1], ", Round 2: ", sent_by_round$sent[2],
                        "). Open circles: total returned to date, including undated returns.\n",
                        "Dashed line: last dated receipt, ", format(last_receipt, "%d %b %Y"),
                        ". Blood-results data through the 2026-06-22 lab export; later receipts, if any, are not yet in the data.")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Returned letters

- 752 of 150,000 letters (0.50\%) came back undeliverable; the set is final and fully categorized (`returned-letters.csv` in ECLIPS-PAP, with codebook)
- Returns cluster in a handful of named buildings (demolition blocks, student residences) and skew by deprivation: 0.87\% of letters in the most-deprived quintile vs 0.21\% in the least
- Denominator effect is below reporting precision: overall usable rate 5.93 per 1,000 mailed vs 5.96 net of returns (Script 27); no IMD quintile moves more than 2.5\% relatively


# Appendix

## Sampling Design: Exclusion / De-duplication

\hypertarget{appx-exclusion}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-datasources}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

\footnotesize

\vspace{-2em }
### Addresses Excluded: 5,521 total (categories overlap)
- Unconventional households: 1,863 (hotels / student residences 936, vacant properties 931, with overlapping tags)
- Missing LSOA: 524 (no valid UPRN $\Rightarrow$ no ONS lookup; includes 73 rows with no UPRN at all)
- Missing postcode (after EPC fallback): 3,491
- Known gap: $\approx$ 87 mailed student addresses missed by the keyword list (Kirkstall Brewery, Lupton Flats); see `sampling-frame-codebook.md`

### De-duplication
- After exclusion, 18 UPRNs appear *exactly twice*  
- These represent errors in the Council Tax data
- Only affects address; keep first one from each pair

### Final Sampling Frame: 370,666 rows
- Address 1-4, postcode, UPRN
- OA (2,607), LSOA (488), MSOA (107)
- EPC Housing age: "Old" if $\leq 1966$ (missing for 38.5\%)
- \# Households in LSOA $\geq$ one child aged $\leq 9$ (2021 Census)

## What is the Index of Multiple Deprivation (IMD)?

\hypertarget{appx-imd}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-deprivation}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

- Official England-wide deprivation measure, published by the Ministry of Housing, Communities \& Local Government; scored for each LSOA ($\approx$ 1,500 residents)
- Combines **seven domains**: income; employment; education \& skills; health \& disability; crime; barriers to housing \& services; living environment
- Higher score = more deprived; we use **IMD 2025** (2021 LSOA boundaries, matching our Census)
- Here: rank Leeds LSOAs by deprivation and split into **quintiles** (equal fifths of LSOAs; Q1 = least, Q5 = most deprived)

## Distribution of LSOA deprivation: England vs Leeds

\hypertarget{appx-imd-dist}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-deprivation}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

\vspace{-2em}
:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="56%"}

```{r}
#| label: imd-england-vs-leeds
#| fig-width: 4.8
#| fig-height: 4.8
#| out-width: 100%
library(patchwork)

# England (all LSOAs, including Leeds) vs Leeds alone, on a shared score axis.
eng   <- read_csv("../data/england-lsoa-imd-2025-scores.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
xlims <- range(eng$imd_score)

dens <- function(df, ttl, fillcol, xlab = NULL) {
  ggplot(df, aes(imd_score)) +
    geom_density(fill = fillcol, colour = NA) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = xlims) +
    labs(title = ttl, x = xlab, y = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(size = 12))
}

dens(eng, "England — 33,755 LSOAs", "grey65") /
  dens(filter(eng, lsoa %in% lsoa_imd$lsoa), "Leeds — 488 LSOAs", COL_ORANGE,
       xlab = "IMD score  (higher = more deprived)")
```

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

\vspace{2em}

\footnotesize

```{r}
#| label: imd-england-vs-leeds-table
#| results: asis
tab <- read_csv("../data/england-lsoa-imd-2025-scores.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(leeds = lsoa %in% lsoa_imd$lsoa, dec = ntile(-imd_score, 10))

fmt <- function(d) c(
  sprintf("%.1f", median(d$imd_score)),
  sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * mean(d$dec == 1)),
  sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * mean(d$dec == 10)),
  sprintf("%.0f%%", 100 * mean(d$dec %in% 4:7)))

tibble(
  Measure = c("Median IMD score",
              "Most-deprived decile",
              "Least-deprived decile",
              "Middle (deciles 4--7)"),
  England = fmt(tab),
  Leeds   = fmt(filter(tab, leeds))
) |>
  knitr::kable(format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, align = "lrr", linesep = "",
               col.names = c("", "England", "Leeds"))
```

:::

::::

## Quintiles of Deprivation in Leeds: by LSOA

\hypertarget{appx-imd-map}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-deprivation}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

\vspace{-2em}

```{r}
#| label: leeds-imd-map
#| fig-width: 6.6
#| fig-height: 4
leeds_lsoa <- leeds_geo |>
  left_join(lsoa_imd, by = c("LSOA21CD" = "lsoa")) |>
  mutate(quintile = factor(bin_imd(imd_score),
                           labels = c("Q1 least", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5 most")))

ggplot(leeds_lsoa, aes(fill = quintile)) +
  geom_sf(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.05) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlOrBr", direction = 1, na.value = "grey85",
                    name = "IMD quintile") +
  coord_sf(datum = NA) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

## Heterogeneity: does the gradient differ by treatment? (Old)

\hypertarget{appx-imd-treat}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-by-age}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

```{r}
#| label: imd-by-treatment
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.5
imd_cells |>
  filter(age_grp == "Old") |>
  imd_summary("treatment") |>
  mutate(treatment = factor(TREAT_LABELS[treatment],
                            levels = unname(TREAT_LABELS))) |>
  gradient_plot("treatment", TREAT_COLS, "Usable per 1,000",
                caption = paste0("Old houses only; ", ci_caption))
```

\footnotesize \hyperlink{appx-click-treat}{\beamergotobutton{Clicks version of this plot}}


## Empirical Bayes Estimation of LSOA Response Rates 

For LSOA $\ell$ with $n_\ell$ letters and $y_\ell$ usable responses:

### Model
$y_\ell \mid p_\ell \sim \text{Binomial}(n_\ell, p_\ell), \qquad p_\ell \sim \text{Beta}(a, b)$ — the shared prior is what lets sparse areas borrow strength.


### Conjugate Posterior
**Posterior** (conjugate). 
$p_\ell \mid y_\ell \sim \text{Beta}(y_\ell + a,\ n_\ell - y_\ell + b)$, so

$$\hat p_\ell = \frac{y_\ell + a}{n_\ell + a + b} = w_\ell\,\frac{y_\ell}{n_\ell} + (1 - w_\ell)\,\frac{a}{a + b}, \qquad w_\ell = \frac{n_\ell}{n_\ell + a + b}$$


### Estimation
Choose $(a, b)$ to maximise the marginal Beta-Binomial likelihood $\;\prod_\ell \int \text{Bin}(y_\ell \mid n_\ell, p)\,\text{Beta}(p \mid a, b)\,dp\;$, then plug in: here $\hat a \approx$ `r round(eb_a)`, $\hat b \approx$ `r round(eb_b)`.

## Shrinkage of LSOA estimates, and the estimated rate distribution

```{r}
#| label: eb-shrinkage
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.6
#| out-width: 88%
library(patchwork)
xmax <- max(eb_lsoa$raw)

funnel <- ggplot(eb_lsoa) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = raw, xend = post, y = 1, yend = 0),
               colour = COL_BLUE, alpha = 0.12, linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_point(aes(raw,  1), colour = COL_ORANGE, size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(post, 0), colour = COL_BLUE, size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 1, label = "raw  y/n", hjust = 0, vjust = -1.1, size = 3.3) +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "shrunk",   hjust = 0, vjust = 2.1,  size = 3.3) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, xmax)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.4, 1.4), breaks = NULL) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())

prior <- tibble(rate = seq(0, xmax, length.out = 400)) |>
  mutate(dens = dbeta(rate / 1000, eb_a, eb_b))
distp <- ggplot(prior, aes(rate, dens)) +
  geom_area(fill = COL_BLUE, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, xmax)) +
  labs(x = "Usable per 1,000", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank())

funnel / distp + plot_layout(heights = c(1.1, 1))
```

\vspace{-1em}

\small Top: each LSOA's raw rate (upper row) joined to its empirical-Bayes estimate (lower row). Bottom: the fitted Beta prior --- the estimated distribution of true rates.

## Usable response rate by LSOA: raw

\hypertarget{appx-eb}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-deprivation}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

```{r}
#| label: eb-map-raw
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4.2
#| out-width: 62%
lsoa_map(eb_lsoa, "raw", "Usable\nper 1,000", option = "rocket")
```

\small $y/n$ per LSOA: 0--`r round(max(eb_lsoa$raw))` per 1,000, with `r sum(eb_lsoa$y == 0)` of `r nrow(eb_lsoa)` LSOAs at zero --- mostly sampling noise.

## Usable response rate by LSOA: empirical-Bayes shrunk

```{r}
#| label: eb-map-shrunk
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4.2
#| out-width: 62%
lsoa_map(eb_lsoa, "post", "Usable\nper 1,000")
```

\small Posterior means --- *own scale (`r round(min(eb_lsoa$post))`--`r round(max(eb_lsoa$post))`) and a different palette*. Far narrower range: most LSOAs pulled to the Leeds average.

## Response vs. eligible share, with empirical-Bayes rates

```{r}
#| label: elig-share-scatter-eb
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 3.5
# Appendix companion to "Usable response rises only weakly with the LSOA's
# eligible share": dots are the Beta-Binomial posterior means (the same
# shrinkage as the LSOA maps) instead of raw rates, taming the small-LSOA
# noise. The line is unchanged -- the WLS fit on the raw rates.
eb_share <- eb_lsoa |>
  left_join(lsoa_elig_tab |> select(lsoa, share, q_lab, letters), by = "lsoa")

ggplot(eb_share, aes(share, post)) +
  geom_smooth(data = lsoa_elig_tab, aes(share, rate, weight = letters),
              method = "lm", formula = y ~ x,
              colour = "grey30", linewidth = 0.7, fill = "grey80") +
  geom_point(aes(size = letters, fill = q_lab), shape = 21,
             colour = "grey35", stroke = 0.2, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "YlOrBr", name = "IMD quintile") +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 4, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Census share of households with a young child (LSOA)",
       y = "Usable per 1,000 (EB posterior)",
       caption = paste0("Dots: Beta-Binomial posterior means per LSOA (the shrinkage behind the appendix maps); dot area = letters mailed.\n",
                        "Line: the same WLS fit as the main slide, computed from the raw rates with letters weights.")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey40"))
```

## Clicking: deprivation gradient by housing age

\hypertarget{appx-click-age}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{main-by-age}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

```{r}
#| label: click-imd-by-age
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.3
imd_summary(imd_cells, "age_grp", outcome = "clicks") |>
  mutate(age_grp = factor(age_grp, levels = c("Old", "New", "Unknown"))) |>
  gradient_plot("age_grp", AGE_COLS, "Clicks per 1,000")
```

## Clicking: does the gradient differ by treatment? (Old)

\hypertarget{appx-click-treat}{}

\hfill\hyperlink{appx-imd-treat}{\beamerreturnbutton{Back}}

```{r}
#| label: click-imd-by-treatment
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 3.3
imd_cells |>
  filter(age_grp == "Old") |>
  imd_summary("treatment", outcome = "clicks") |>
  mutate(treatment = factor(TREAT_LABELS[treatment],
                            levels = unname(TREAT_LABELS))) |>
  gradient_plot("treatment", TREAT_COLS, "Clicks per 1,000",
                caption = paste0("Old houses only; ", ci_caption))
```

## Spillovers from local letter density: checked; the data cannot answer it

\hypertarget{appx-spillover}{}

\small

### The idea
- Within an LSOA, *which* addresses got letters was a lottery, so nearby Output Areas got different shares by chance; if neighbors talk, more-saturated OAs should respond at a higher rate per letter

### Why the data cannot answer it
- OAs are census units of $\approx$ 94--211 addresses, so the chance variation is tiny: SD 3.5 pp of saturation, $\approx$ 5 letters among 150 addresses
- Detectable slope (80% power): 2--3% relative change in response per +1 pp of neighbors mailed --- linearly, mailing all vs. none would have to *at least triple* response
- A large-but-plausible spillover (full saturation raising response 25%) is $\approx 10\times$ too small to detect

### The result (all specifications prespecified)
- Null: started $-1.0\%$ [$-5.7$, $+3.7$], usable $-1.1\%$ [$-7.7$, $+5.6$] per +3.5 pp (one SD)
- Reading: no steep hyper-local dose-response; uninformative about gentle slopes

\footnotesize Full record: `saturation-spillover-design-note.md`, Script 28, `spillover-estimates.csv` (ECLIPS-PAP).
